verses.1: A gentle answer turns away wrath,
verses.2: The tongue of the wise commends knowledge,
verses.4: A gentle tongue is a tree of life,
verses.5: A fool despises his father’s correction,
verses.6: In the house of the righteous is much treasure,
verses.7: The lips of the wise spread knowledge;
verses.8: The sacrifice made by the wicked is an abomination to the LORD,
verses.9: The way of the wicked is an abomination to the LORD,
verses.10: There is stern discipline for one who forsakes the way.
verses.11: Sheol and Abaddon are before the LORD—
verses.12: A scoffer doesn’t love to be reproved;
verses.13: A glad heart makes a cheerful face,
verses.14: The heart of one who has understanding seeks knowledge,
verses.15: All the days of the afflicted are wretched,
verses.16: Better is little, with the fear of the LORD,
verses.17: Better is a dinner of herbs, where love is,
verses.18: A wrathful man stirs up contention,
verses.19: The way of the sluggard is like a thorn patch,
verses.20: A wise son makes a father glad,
verses.21: Folly is joy to one who is void of wisdom,
verses.22: Where there is no counsel, plans fail;
verses.23: Joy comes to a man with the reply of his mouth.
verses.24: The path of life leads upward for the wise,
verses.25: The LORD will uproot the house of the proud,
verses.26: The LORD detests the thoughts of the wicked,
verses.27: He who is greedy for gain troubles his own house,
verses.28: The heart of the righteous weighs answers,
verses.29: The LORD is far from the wicked,
verses.30: The light of the eyes rejoices the heart.
verses.31: The ear that listens to reproof lives,
verses.32: He who refuses correction despises his own soul,
verses.33: The fear of the LORD teaches wisdom.
